message:
chore(beads): add 3 REVIEW-A8 graphql bugs (subscription wrong-id, batch reorder, dedup ignores idempotent)

Records three independently reproducible graphql client/subscription bugs
surfaced during the REVIEW-A8 audit pass. All three are real bugs that
can be triggered by a misbehaving (or compromised) server/intermediary
or by ordinary mixed-idempotence concurrent callers, and none are
covered by existing regression tests.

- flywheel_connectors-3fo16 [MEDIUM] fcp-graphql/subscription.rs:218:
  the receive loop matches only on message_type and never validates
  ws_msg.id against the fixed SUBSCRIPTION_ID before forwarding next/
  error/complete frames. A server replaying a stale frame with
  id="stale-99" will be accepted as if it belonged to the active
  subscription. Suggested fix: require ws_msg.id ==
  Some(SUBSCRIPTION_ID.to_string()) for next/error/complete frames and
  add regression coverage.

- flywheel_connectors-8wjj5 [MEDIUM] fcp-graphql/client.rs:625:
  decode_batch_response only checks Vec length equality; an
  equal-length but reordered or duplicated response array passes
  silently. The public batch API returns Vec<GraphqlResponse<_>> in
  request order so callers naturally associate response[i] with
  input[i], but that association is currently unchecked. Note: the
  earlier 11bc9b95 fix only closed the short-response underflow case.
  Suggested fix: fail closed on uncorrelatable multi-item batches or
  introduce/require a per-item correlation field.

- flywheel_connectors-brq7n [HIGH] fcp-graphql/client.rs:659: dedup
  ignores the idempotent flag — execute_bytes() keys the in-flight map
  only on body_bytes, so two concurrent execute_request calls with
  identical bodies but different idempotent flags share the FIRST
  caller's Shared future and inherit its retry semantics. A
  non-idempotent caller can hitchhike on a retried transport
  operation, or an idempotent caller can have its retries suppressed.
  This crosses an operation-safety boundary because execute_request
  is a public API and dedup_in_flight is a client-wide toggle.
  Suggested fix: include retry semantics (at minimum the idempotent
  flag) in the dedup key, or refuse dedup when concurrent callers
  disagree on retryability.

All three were filed with reproducer steps, "why this is real"
justifications, and concrete fix suggestions to make them ready to pick
up.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3fdt","title":"[LC] Host rollout controller + health gating","description":"# [LC] Host rollout controller + health gating

## Goal
Implement staged rollout logic in fcp-host with health-based rollback and audit events.

## Deliverables
- Rollout scheduler (canary -> full) per connector.
- Health evaluation hook (self-check + error rates + uptime).
- Automatic rollback logic with audit event and evidence bundle.
- State machine with deterministic transitions and persistence.

## Tests
- Unit tests for state transitions and rollback triggers.
- Integration tests with test connectors simulating health regressions.
- Structured JSON logs for rollout decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Canary success promotes to full rollout; failure triggers rollback.
- Pinning prevents auto-upgrade.
- Audit events emitted for each transition.","notes":"2026-03-07 CoralStream: wired rollout controller into the live fcp-host binary, added /rpc/rollout schedule/evaluate/status endpoints, and verified with rch exec cargo clippy -p fcp-host --all-targets -- -D warnings plus rch exec cargo test -p fcp-host. Added binary-level rollout promotion coverage in host_connector_integration.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:03:30.996398241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T20:58:13.054975Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T20:58:13.054958Z","close_reason":"Rollout controller slice complete; remaining dependency is parent host feature oip0","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3fdt","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-334n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3fdt","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-oip0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3fdt","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-s9t8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":983,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3fdt","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Implementation complete in commit 93d1d505. Rollout controller (rollout.rs) includes: canary scheduling, health-gated evaluation, auto-rollback with deterministic evidence bundles, crash-loop detection, pinning, structured JSON audit events. 48 unit tests covering all transitions. Blocked by oip0 (host gateway) which is separately in progress.","created_at":"2026-03-07T16:19:28Z"},{"id":1019,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3fdt","author":"GoldOx","text":"Rollout controller acceptance criteria are now covered end-to-end on the live host path. Existing rollout.rs unit tests already covered promotion, rollback, pinning, and deterministic evidence bundles; this session added binary integration coverage for schedule -> failed evaluate -> automatic rollback plus structured endpoint/decision logs, and reverified with rch exec cargo clippy -p fcp-host --all-targets -- -D warnings, rch exec cargo test -p fcp-host --all-targets, and rch exec cargo check --workspace --all-targets. Parent host bead flywheel_connectors-oip0 remains open, but the rollout-controller slice itself now appears complete.","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:58:09Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-3fo16","title":"[REVIEW-A8][MEDIUM] fcp-graphql/subscription.rs:218: subscription stream accepts frames for the wrong operation id","description":"files: crates/fcp-graphql/src/subscription.rs:29, 217-261, 367-370

repro:
1. Start a subscription normally so the client sends subscribe with SUBSCRIPTION_ID = "1".
2. Have the server send a `next`, `error`, or `complete` message with `id: "stale-99"` (or any id other than "1").
3. The receive loop matches only on message_type and never validates ws_msg.id against SUBSCRIPTION_ID before forwarding data/errors or terminating the stream.
4. The stream accepts the foreign frame as if it belonged to the active subscription.

why this is real:
- The client uses a fixed logical subscription id on the wire, so inbound id checking is the only guard against stale or foreign frames.
- This is especially brittle around reconnect/late-frame edges: a server or intermediary that replays a stale frame with the wrong id can still affect the live stream.

suggested fix:
Require `ws_msg.id == Some(SUBSCRIPTION_ID.to_string())` for `next`, `error`, and `complete` frames; reject or ignore mismatched ids with a protocol error, and add regression coverage for wrong-id `next` / `error` / `complete` messages.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:03.601779Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:03.601779Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3gzz","title":"[SC] Tests: unit + integration","description":"# [SC] Tests: unit + integration

## Goal
Comprehensive unit + integration coverage for attestation/SBOM verification and policy gating.

## Unit Tests
- Valid attestation/SBOM passes.
- Missing or invalid signature fails with correct error code.
- SBOM hash mismatch fails.
- Attestation predicate digest mismatch fails.
- Policy overrides (allow_unsigned, min_slsa_level) behave as configured.

## Integration Tests
- Host install gating with test connector bundles:
  - Valid bundle installs.
  - Invalid bundle rejected.
  - Policy override allows dev-only bundle.

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs for each verification step (stage, input digest, result, duration_ms).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover success/failure paths with deterministic outputs.
- Logs are stable and redact sensitive fields.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

Adding comprehensive fcp-core/src/supply_chain.rs unit tests for all validation paths, deterministic hashing, signing bytes, and helper functions.","created_at":"2026-03-01T21:05:56Z"},{"id":312,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3gzz","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Completed comprehensive unit test suite for fcp-core/src/supply_chain.rs. Added 86 new tests (93 total, up from 7). Coverage includes:
- Attestation validation: wrong format, schema version, empty fields, invalid digests (prefix/algo/length/case), empty materials, bad material URIs/digests, metadata time ordering, invocation_id, slsa_level boundaries (0-5), builder_allowlist rules, trust root types (sigstore/tuf/manual/invalid), signature algorithm/key_id/signed_fields
- SBOM validation: wrong format, schema version, empty bom_version/tool_chain/components, component fields (id/name/version/hashes/licenses), unsupported hash algos, duplicate component_ids, dependency graph (empty/unknown refs, duplicates, empty targets), trust root and signature fields
- Deterministic encoding: CBOR/JSON canonical bytes, content hashing (blake3-256/sha256), signing bytes with domain separation, cross-encoding hash difference
- Serde round-trips: attestation/sbom JSON round-trip, predicate type URL serialization, SPDX format
- Helper functions: is_lower_hex_64 (empty/short/long/uppercase/non-hex), digest_is_supported (blake3/sha256/unknown/no-colon), hash_bytes output format, HashAlgorithm::as_str/default
- Cross-cutting: attestation vs SBOM signing bytes use different domains, error Display messages for all 5 variants
Quality gates: cargo test (93/93 pass), cargo clippy -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check (clean).","created_at":"2026-03-01T21:18:25Z"},{"id":993,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3gzz","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Added 18 integration tests in fcp-host/tests/supply_chain_integration.rs covering: happy path, deny missing attestation, deny missing SBOM, SLSA level gating, untrusted builder, digest mismatch, dev override, cache integration (serve/isolate/clear), deterministic evidence, audit events (cache hit/miss), JSON serialization round-trip, and SLSA boundary policy matrix. All 18 tests pass. Combined with the 86 core unit tests, this bead's acceptance criteria are fully met.","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:21:29Z"},{"id":1015,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3gzz","author":"GoldOx","text":"Re-verified the supply-chain test bead against the current fcp-host worktree.

Fresh verification in this session:
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.target-goldox-host-cache cargo test -p fcp-host --all-targets
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.target-goldox-host-cache cargo clippy -p fcp-host --all-targets -- -D warnings

Observed coverage on disk:
- host-side verification unit coverage in crates/fcp-host/src/supply_chain.rs
- 18 integration tests in crates/fcp-host/tests/supply_chain_integration.rs covering allow/deny paths, policy overrides, cache behavior, deterministic evidence, audit-event cache-hit behavior, and serialization round-trips

Important scope note: I am closing only this test bead. The broader host verification pipeline bead remains open because the current code search shows SupplyChainGate exists as a host module with tests, but it is not yet wired into a broader install/upgrade flow.
","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:27:03Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8v34","title":"TEST: Enrollment Unit Tests (DeviceEnrollment, NodeKeyAttestation, Removal Workflow)","description":"# TEST: Enrollment Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that device enrollment and removal work correctly and securely.

## Test Categories

### DeviceEnrollment Object
- [ ] Valid enrollment parses correctly
- [ ] Owner signature verifies
- [ ] Tailscale node_id binding correct
- [ ] Authorized tags match
- [ ] Expiry (if present) enforced
- [ ] Malformed enrollment rejected

### NodeKeyAttestation
- [ ] Attestation binds node_id to pubkeys
- [ ] signing_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] encryption_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] issuance_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] KID mappings correct
- [ ] Owner signature verifies
- [ ] Attestation expiry enforced

### Enrollment Flow
- [ ] New node can enroll with valid enrollment object
- [ ] Enrolled node receives zone keys (ZoneKeyManifest)
- [ ] Enrolled node can participate in sessions
- [ ] Enrolled node appears in peer discovery

### Key Rotation
- [ ] Node can rotate its keys
- [ ] New attestation replaces old
- [ ] Old KIDs become invalid
- [ ] Sessions using old keys gracefully terminated

### Device Removal
- [ ] Removal revokes enrollment object
- [ ] Removal revokes attestation
- [ ] Removal revokes issuer keys
- [ ] Tokens from removed node rejected
- [ ] Zone keys rotated after removal
- [ ] Removed node cannot participate

### Attack Scenarios (Adversarial)
- [ ] Replay old enrollment: rejected (revoked or expired)
- [ ] Forge enrollment: invalid owner signature rejected
- [ ] Use removed node keys: rejected after revocation propagates
- [ ] Impersonate node_id: attestation signature check fails

## Golden Vectors
- Valid DeviceEnrollment bytes
- Valid NodeKeyAttestation bytes
- Removal workflow sequence

## Integration Tests
- [ ] Full enrollment → operation → removal cycle
- [ ] Multi-node enrollment
- [ ] Concurrent enrollment attempts

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "node_id": "...",
  "operation": "enroll|attest|rotate|remove",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Enrollment flow works end-to-end
- Removal flow invalidates all node authority
- No stale credentials accepted after removal

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8w4","title":"fcp.kubernetes: Exec into pods for debugging and maintenance","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: Exec into pods for debugging and maintenance (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a controlled exec surface for debugging:
- run a constrained command in a container

Exec is **high risk** (arbitrary code execution in prod). Must be approval gated and heavily audited.

## Scope
- Start an exec session with:
  - explicit namespace/pod/container
  - explicit command allowlist (policy-owned)
  - explicit timeout
- Capture session transcript metadata (not raw output by default).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `k8s.exec` (critical)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Approval gating recommended default for exec.
- Audit:
  - emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent including command hash + targets
  - never log secrets/output by default

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `k8s.exec.start`
  - `k8s.exec.status`
  - `k8s.exec.cancel`
- Safety layer:
  - deny interactive shells by default
  - deny dangerous commands unless explicitly allowed

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - allowlist enforcement
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Exec cannot run without explicit authorization and is fully auditable.

Kubernetes connector now at 28 ops, 322 tests. Committed ec5c4e4d.","created_at":"2026-03-09T08:19:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8whb3","title":"[MOR] Epic: Type-Level Security Enforcement — Phantom Types, Sealed Traits, Type-State","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:54.049698Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:51:11.163795Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T23:51:11.163050Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["MOR-finding-C4","security","type-safety"],"comments":[{"id":2639,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8whb3","author":"jemanuel","text":"MODES-OF-REASONING FINDING C4 — Confidence: 0.88 — Supporting modes: A7 (Type-Theoretic), J1 (Deontic), H2 (Adversarial-Review), F7 (Systems-Thinking). PROBLEM: Critical security obligations are stated in prose (README, spec) but not enforced by Rusts type system or trait boundaries. The connector trait allows bypassing capability verification, zone binding is not type-encoded, taint reduction has no proof-carrying requirement, and the default simulate() returns allowed for all operations. EVIDENCE: (1) A7: 0 of 3 major state machines are type-encoded. Zone boundaries runtime-checked. Token verification optional. Provenance lattice invariants are runtime merge functions, not types. Identified 4 phantom-type opportunities with code sketches. (2) J1: No Cross-Connector Calling is documented only, no mechanical enforcement. simulate() default returns allowed — violates default-deny. Capability constraints optional (null = unlimited). (3) H2: Capability ceiling bypass (null constraints = unlimited scope). Holder-binding enforcement unclear. Delegation depth not validated. (4) F7: Without automatic conformance enforcement, each connector deviation is small but interactions create cascade points. KEY FILES: crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs, crates/fcp-core/src/revocation.rs, crates/fcp-core/src/connector.rs, crates/fcp-sdk/src/lib.rs, crates/fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs. ACCEPTANCE: Phantom types enforce token verification at compile time. Taint reduction requires proof. Lifecycle transitions are compile-time checked. Null constraints rejected. Default simulate() denies. Connector traits sealed.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:36:47Z"},{"id":2664,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8whb3","author":"jemanuel","text":"All 7 C3.x children closed: C3.1 (phantom types), C3.2 (proof-carrying taint), C3.3 (type-state lifecycle), C3.4 (mandatory constraints), C3.5 (default-deny simulate), C3.6 (sealed traits), C3.7 (ZoneBound<T>). Epic complete.","created_at":"2026-04-10T23:51:04Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-8wjj5","title":"[REVIEW-A8][MEDIUM] fcp-graphql/client.rs:625: batch client silently accepts reordered or duplicated equal-length responses","description":"files: crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:600-648, crates/fcp-graphql/src/operation.rs:98-107

repro:
1. Build a batch with two distinct variable payloads via GraphqlClient::execute_batch / execute_batch_request.
2. Have the server return a two-element JSON array with the same length as the request, but reverse the responses or duplicate the first response twice.
3. decode_batch_response() only deserializes Vec<GraphqlResponse<R>> and checks len == expected_responses.
4. Because GraphqlBatchItem carries no per-item correlation id, the client returns the reordered/duplicated vector as if element N still belonged to request N.

why this is real:
- 11bc9b95 only fixed short-response underflow. Equal-length permutations still pass silently.
- The public batch API returns Vec<GraphqlResponse<_>> in request order, so callers naturally associate response[i] with input[i]. Today that association is unchecked and can be wrong.

suggested fix:
Fail closed on uncorrelatable multi-item batches instead of silently trusting positional correlation, or introduce/require an echoed per-item correlation field (for example via a client-generated extension/index that is validated on response). At minimum add a regression test with a reversed equal-length response so this silent misassociation is visible in CI.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:03.472870Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:03.472870Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8yxg","title":"fcp.arxiv: Research Literature and Paper Access Connector","description":"# fcp.arxiv - Research Literature and Paper Access Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Plan and implement an FCP2-compliant arXiv connector for literature search and paper retrieval (metadata + PDF) under safe NetworkConstraints and input validation.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 78/100** — Specialized Tier 3 connector (Research family).

**Why Critical**: ArXiv is the **primary preprint server for STEM research**, hosting 2M+ papers. Essential for research agents, AI/ML practitioners, and anyone needing access to cutting-edge scientific literature. Enables autonomous research workflows.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Specialized Investment (Research + Domain Knowledge)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 3 Enterprise (weeks 9-12)

### Semantic Scholar Comparison
- **ArXiv**: Raw preprints, full PDF access, direct source
- **Semantic Scholar**: Citation graphs, abstracts, AI-enhanced search
- Both needed for comprehensive research access

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Search & Discovery
- Query papers by topic, author, date
- Category-based filtering (cs.AI, cs.LG, etc.)
- Full-text search via abstract
- Citation tracking

### Content Access
- Download PDFs
- Access paper metadata
- Get LaTeX source (when available)
- Version history

### Research Workflows
- Literature review automation
- Citation network exploration
- Trend analysis across time
- New paper alerts

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Find recent papers on transformer architectures"
"Download papers cited by this one"
"What's new in cs.AI this week?"
"Find all papers by this author"
"Summarize the abstract of this paper"
"Track citations for these foundational papers"
"Compare methods across these papers"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### CASS (Context-Aware Semantic Search)
**Research knowledge base:**
- Index paper abstracts
- Semantic search over literature
- Build research corpus

### Vector DB Integration
- Paper embeddings for similarity search
- Find related work automatically
- Cluster papers by topic

### AI Provider Integration
- Summarize papers with LLMs
- Extract key findings
- Generate literature reviews

### Agent Mail Integration
- Research findings → agent threads
- Collaborative literature review
- Share paper recommendations

### Notion/Airtable Integration
- Track reading lists
- Annotate papers
- Organize research projects

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
ArXiv provides OAI-PMH and REST-like access:

- **Search API**: `export.arxiv.org/api/query`
- **OAI-PMH**: Metadata harvesting
- **PDF Access**: Direct download via arxiv.org

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `quick-xml` — Atom/XML parsing
- `url` — URL construction
- `regex` — ArXiv ID parsing

### ArXiv ID Format
```
Old: hep-th/9901001
New: 2301.12345
Version: 2301.12345v2
```

### Response Format (Atom)
```xml
<entry>
  <id>http://arxiv.org/abs/2301.12345v1</id>
  <title>Paper Title</title>
  <summary>Abstract text...</summary>
  <author><name>Author Name</name></author>
  <category term="cs.AI" scheme="http://arxiv.org/schemas/atom"/>
  <published>2023-01-25T00:00:00Z</published>
</entry>
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:research:arxiv:full` | Full access | Search and download |
| `z:research:arxiv:search` | Search only | Metadata access |
| `z:research:arxiv:category:<cat>` | Category-scoped | e.g., cs.AI only |

### Data Considerations
- Papers are public — low sensitivity
- Download volume should be respectful
- Bulk access has rate limits

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — REST API operations

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `export.arxiv.org:443` (API)
- `arxiv.org:443` (PDF download)

### Credential Injection
- No authentication required
- Rate limiting is IP-based
- Respect robots.txt

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `arxiv.search` — Query papers
- `arxiv.metadata` — Get paper details
- `arxiv.pdf.download` — Download PDF
- `arxiv.source.download` — Download LaTeX source

### No Write Operations
ArXiv is read-only for consumers.

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Search Papers
```
GET /api/query?
  search_query=all:transformer+AND+cat:cs.AI
  &start=0
  &max_results=10
  &sortBy=submittedDate
  &sortOrder=descending
```

### Get Paper by ID
```
GET /api/query?id_list=2301.12345
```

### Download PDF
```
GET https://arxiv.org/pdf/2301.12345.pdf
```

### Category Codes
| Code | Field |
|------|-------|
| cs.AI | Artificial Intelligence |
| cs.LG | Machine Learning |
| cs.CL | Computation and Language (NLP) |
| cs.CV | Computer Vision |
| stat.ML | Statistics - ML |
| math.* | Mathematics |
| physics.* | Physics |

---

## Research Workflow Patterns

### Literature Review
1. Search for topic keywords
2. Retrieve top N papers
3. Extract abstracts
4. Use LLM to summarize findings
5. Identify citation chains
6. Build reading list

### New Paper Monitoring
1. Query recent submissions by category
2. Filter by keywords/authors
3. Generate daily digest
4. Track citation counts over time

### Citation Network Analysis
1. Start with seed papers
2. Extract references
3. Recursively fetch cited papers
4. Build citation graph
5. Identify influential works

---

## Rate Limiting

### ArXiv Policies
- 1 request per 3 seconds recommended
- Bulk downloads require coordination
- Respect robots.txt
- Consider using data dumps for large-scale analysis

### Implementation
```rust
// Rate limiter: max 20 requests per minute
let limiter = RateLimiter::new(20, Duration::from_secs(60));
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Search query construction
- Atom feed parsing
- ArXiv ID validation
- Category handling

### Integration Tests
- wiremock-based API simulation
- Pagination testing
- Multi-paper fetch scenarios

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Rate limit enforcement
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real ArXiv calls in unit tests
- Search queries functional
- PDF download works
- Atom response parsing correct
- Rate limiting implemented
- Category filtering works

## Success Criteria
- Connector epic is fully decomposed into implementable beads (manifest/cap map, provisioning, operations, tests, E2E compliance).
- All child beads are implemented and closed, and the connector passes the mechanical compliance runner + its E2E scenarios with structured JSON logs.
- Mechanical guarantees hold: single-zone binding, default-deny capabilities, and NetworkConstraints enforcement; no secret/PII leakage in logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8yxg.1","title":"fcp.arxiv: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.arxiv: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the arXiv connector’s **mechanical manifest contract** under FCP2.

## Required contents
- Manifest archetypes (closed set):
  - `knowledge` (search/retrieve papers)
  - `operational` (monitors/alerts configuration)
  - `streaming` (polling-backed event stream for new papers)
- Operation list + schema IDs.
- Capability families + per-operation required caps.
- NetworkConstraints:
  - pin arXiv API hosts
  - pin PDF download hosts
  - enforce strict rate limits to avoid bans
- State model declaration:
  - monitor cursors/checkpoints for polling
  - cached metadata rules (must be bounded)

## Acceptance criteria
- Another engineer can implement `--manifest` from this bead alone.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8yxg.2","title":"fcp.arxiv: Provisioning Automation (rate limits, monitors, optional enrichments)","description":"# fcp.arxiv: Provisioning Automation (rate limits, monitors, optional enrichments)

## Goal
Automate arXiv connector onboarding with minimal human steps.

arXiv is mostly open-access, but provisioning still matters for:
- rate limiting configuration (avoid IP bans)
- monitor/alert setup
- optional enrichments (CASS indexing, semantic search)

## Requirements
- Configure and validate:
  - base API host(s)
  - strict rate limits (token bucket; 3 req/sec baseline)
- Monitor setup automation:
  - create/list/delete monitors
  - store polling checkpoints in connector state (if enabled)
- Optional enrichment hooks (policy-owned):
  - CASS indexing configuration
  - embedding provider selection for semantic search

## Acceptance criteria
- `fcp doctor` verifies readiness (host reachability + rate limiter sanity) without secrets.
- Mockable in CI.

+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-brq7n","title":"[REVIEW-A8][HIGH] fcp-graphql/client.rs:659: dedup ignores idempotent flag and can replay non-idempotent requests","description":"files: crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:497-504, 651-718

repro:
1. Configure one GraphqlClient with dedup_in_flight=true and a retry policy that retries transport failures.
2. Issue two concurrent execute_request(...) calls with byte-identical bodies but different idempotent flags (the method is public and takes `idempotent: bool` explicitly).
3. execute_bytes() keys the in-flight map only on body_bytes and returns any existing Shared future before considering the second caller's idempotence.
4. The inserted Shared future captures the FIRST caller's idempotent flag in send_with_retry(payload, idempotent).
5. If the first caller used idempotent=true and the second used idempotent=false, the non-idempotent caller hitchhikes on a retried transport operation after a partial failure. Reversing the order suppresses retries for the idempotent caller.

why this is real:
- This is not theoretical API misuse: execute_request is public specifically for arbitrary/manual operations, and dedup_in_flight is a client-wide toggle.
- The bug crosses an operation-safety boundary: a non-idempotent request can be replayed because an earlier caller happened to classify the same payload as retryable.

suggested fix:
Include retry semantics in the dedup key (at minimum the idempotent flag, possibly the full retry-policy inputs) or refuse dedup when concurrent callers disagree on retryability. Add a regression test with mixed-idempotence concurrent callers proving the second caller cannot inherit the first caller's retry behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:03.601781Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:13:03.601781Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih","title":"[FCP3-V3] Universal Harmonization: Complete runtime, error, and transport migration","description":"## Goal

Complete the FCP3 V3 universal harmonization across all 89 connectors and core crates. Every connector and infrastructure crate should use the FCP3-native runtime model with no compatibility-first holdouts.

## Background

The FCP3 re-foundation (9syku epic, 56 beads) established the V3 spec, runtime kernel, and migration framework. ConnectorErrorMapping has been adopted by 87/87 connectors (100%). However, only 2/89 connectors use ConnectorRuntime lifecycle and RetryLoop. Additionally, 6 core crates bypass fcp-async-core by importing asupersync directly, and the Tokio compat bridge remains for test infrastructure.

## What 'done' looks like

1. All 89 connectors use ConnectorRuntime for lifecycle (configure/shutdown)
2. All connectors with retry logic use RetryLoop instead of hand-rolled loops
3. fcp-async-core::io extended with AsyncWrite + lines() support
4. serve_mcp.rs migrated off tokio::io
5. All 6 core crates use fcp-async-core wrappers (no raw asupersync imports)
6. Test infrastructure evaluated for native async mocking
7. Documentation fully reflects V3-only reality

## Reference

- Kill list: docs/FCP3_Retirement_Kill_List.md
- Migration framework: crates/fcp-sdk/src/migration.rs

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.1","title":"[FCP3-V3.1] ConnectorRuntime lifecycle adoption across all 89 connectors","description":"## Goal

Every connector must create a ConnectorRuntime during configure() and use it for request contexts, background contexts, and graceful shutdown. Currently only anthropic and openai have this.

## Why this matters

ConnectorRuntime provides:
- Deadline-aware request contexts (prevents unbounded waits)
- Background context for streaming/polling (cancellation-aware)
- Graceful shutdown propagation
- Structured tracing for all lifecycle events

Without it, connectors use ad-hoc timeout/cancellation patterns that are inconsistent and miss the FCP3 observability contract.

## Migration pattern (from crates/fcp-sdk/src/migration.rs)

In connector struct, add: `runtime: Option<ConnectorRuntime>`

In configure():
```rust
self.runtime = Some(ConnectorRuntime::new(ConnectorRuntimeConfig::default()));
```

In invoke/request paths:
```rust
let ctx = self.runtime.as_ref().unwrap().request_context();
```

In shutdown():
```rust
if let Some(rt) = &self.runtime { rt.shutdown(); }
```

## Acceptance criteria
- All 89 connectors have ConnectorRuntime in their struct
- All request paths use runtime.request_context()
- All shutdown paths call runtime.shutdown()
- All existing tests still pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:36:03.771743Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:21:12.615563Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T00:21:12.615161Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp3","runtime","v3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:36:03.771743Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1636,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"PlumCrane: All 6 children closed. ConnectorRuntime lifecycle adopted across all 89+ connectors: bs5ih.1.1 (GitHub/Slack/Linear/Jira/Notion/Stripe), bs5ih.1.2 (9 Google connectors), bs5ih.1.3 (11 database/storage), bs5ih.1.4 (62 remaining). All compile clean, hand-rolled retries replaced with RetryLoop::execute where present.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:21:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-bs5ih.1.1","title":"[FCP3-V3.1.1] ConnectorRuntime: high-traffic RR connectors (github, slack, linear, jira, notion, stripe)","description":"## Scope
Add ConnectorRuntime to the 6 highest-traffic request-response connectors that already have ConnectorErrorMapping but lack ConnectorRuntime.

Connectors: github, slack, linear, jira, notion, stripe

## Why these first
These are the most commonly invoked connectors. Adding ConnectorRuntime gives them deadline-aware contexts and graceful shutdown — the two biggest operational improvements.

## Pattern
Follow anthropic/openai pattern:
1. Add `runtime: Option<ConnectorRuntime>` to connector struct
2. Create runtime in configure()
3. Use runtime.request_context() in invoke paths
4. Call runtime.shutdown() in shutdown()

## Evidence required
- All existing tests pass
- cargo clippy clean
- No hand-rolled timeout logic remains in migrated paths","notes":"[2026-03-14T23:20:00Z] PurpleFern: Claimed via `bv --robot-triage` and progressed the current tree. `github`, `linear`, `notion`, and `stripe` now have the reqwest-side ConnectorRuntime/RetryLoop path wired with shutdown propagation; `jira` had the remaining obvious incomplete edge, so `add_attachment()` was finished onto RetryLoop and the redundant connector-owned runtime was removed. `slack` was verified already migrated in the current tree, so it is not the remaining blocker for this bead. Shared `HttpRetryConfig::to_retry_policy()` semantics in `crates/fcp-sdk/src/migration.rs` were also corrected so `max_retries` means retries after the initial attempt again.
